exercises.title: Build and Inspect a Grade List
exercises.description: Create a list of 7 grades, then print: the first grade, the last grade, the number of grades, and a slice of the middle 3 grades.
exercises.starterCode:
grades = [72, 85, 91, 68, 88, 95, 74]

# TODO: Print the first grade

# TODO: Print the last grade (use negative index)

# TODO: Print the total number of grades

# TODO: Print the middle 3 grades (index 2, 3, 4) using a slice
hints: Use index 0 to access the first element
Use index -1 to access the last element
Use grades[2:5] to slice elements at index 2, 3, 4

exercises.title: Mutate a Playlist
exercises.description: Start with a playlist of 4 songs. Then: change the second song to 'New Song', append 'Bonus Track', remove the first song, and print the final playlist.
exercises.starterCode:
playlist = ["Song A", "Song B", "Song C", "Song D"]

# TODO: Change the second song (index 1) to 'New Song'

# TODO: Append 'Bonus Track' to the end

# TODO: Remove the first song using del or pop

# TODO: Print the final playlist
hints: playlist[1] = 'New Song' sets the value at index 1
playlist.append('Bonus Track') adds to the end
del playlist[0] or playlist.pop(0) removes the first item

exercises.title: Temperature Analysis
exercises.description: Given a list of daily temperatures, print: the highest temperature, the lowest temperature, the average (rounded to 1 decimal), and how many days were above 25 degrees.
exercises.starterCode:
temps = [22, 28, 31, 19, 25, 33, 27, 21, 30, 24]

# TODO: Print the highest temperature using max()

# TODO: Print the lowest temperature using min()

# TODO: Calculate and print the average (sum / count), rounded to 1 decimal

# TODO: Count and print how many days had temp > 25
# Hint: use a for loop or sum() with a condition
hints: sum(1 for t in temps if t > 25) counts items above 25

warmup.title: Quick Recall: List Basics
warmup.description: What does this code print? Run it to check.
